content:
7 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Left Picture Is My Own Photo July 2026, Top Right Image Credit, Bottom Right Image CreditThere is something about reading a report by any mainstream news source these days that irks my soul. Earlier this week, the San Francisco Standard reported that “San Francisco cops on motorcyle, in SUVs, and foot escorted the protesters…” San Francisco Standard listen up: I’ll do you one better than escort — how about unwanted groupies? That is the phrase I think of when I think of ‘SFPD’ and ‘escorting a protest….’
I only wish I had snapped a picture of the brave cops on motorcycles wearing thick sunglasses escorting us protesters….
To counter the monotony that comes out of many mainstream news discourse, some really good independent media I have been loving recently include ‘IHIP’ news with Jennifer Welch and Angie Sullivan, Kat from ‘Spitfire News,’ Akila Hughes from ‘How Is This Better?,’ and Bored Panda!

Anyways, this past weekend’s stop ai rally that started at open ai was a success in my eyes. The people I talked to — shout out to Michelle, Gretchen, and Theo- were all cool. I am always curious how people come to hear about the rallies, and I learned that the Pride Parade, the No Kings rallies, and Dolores Park were all entry points for people. Someone at the rally had a poster advertising this site called “AI-2040.” It’s a good place to start to learn about the potential future of AI. I have been sifting through the content.
Last weekend, the rallying cry was that we want to pause AI because it’s developing at a rate too fast. We need regulation. We need answers to how we will approach the ethics of AI. And, I agree with this. One of the rallying cries was “it is evitable,” and “a better world is possible.”
It is evitable.
We humans have always been more technologically advanced that socially advanced. In one of the most “developed” countries in the world, we are still arguing about whether an abortion should be legal or not. Meanwhile, AI is playing a role in sentencing people to prison, AI is replacing humans for entry level jobs, AI is deciding whether you get an interview, and AI is doing the interviews. If we continue at this rate, I don’t think the future is bright. I am in no way saying there is anything perfect about our current systems. Our current systems are very broken, however AI will only exacerbate those flaws.
AI is simply a directory encyclopedia of current human knowledge. AI can think — in so much as humans can think. I am unsure if it will be like this forever. Likely not. There are very real problems that humanity has that AI will — at best — not solve and — at worst — exacerbate. Problems such as hiring bias , sexualization of women, misogyny, and racism. I could go on.
At the rally, I had one conversation with someone who would have liked to see us rally at SpaceX, and I completely agree with that. I predict future rallies will be held in relation to Space X. Elon Musk should — and will be — on the receiving end of the Stop AI call. Elon Musk is so many things. So many words come to mind. Prolific sexual abuser, a racist, a neonazi, a lowballer, and a wage thief. Mainstream media would like us to believe that Elon Musk is just an eccentric genius who wants everyone to use starlink and who is doing things no one else is doing. Yeah, right.
Get back to me when grok is doing things for humanity that we actually need like solving poverty, allocating resources equitably, curing diseases, and cleaning up after us humans. In the meantime, I guess grok will be doing things like creating sexual deepfakes, calling Hitler “the man,” and taking about ‘anti-white hate.’ Interestingly, when Grok does give measured criticism, it’s reprimanded by its creator. Note his use of the term ‘legacy media.’ Note my ealier use of the term ‘mainstream media.’ I guess we are all tired of ‘the media’ for very different reasons.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Onto the SFPD….
The SFPD’s main function as far as I know is to protect property. They are not the average person’s friends. I am working on another article where I will be discussing how it is eerie that one of the highest paid jobs accessible to the average person today is a cop, or an ice agent, or a security guard. Just go ahead, google jobs in your city and see what jobs come up. It’s by design. It’s by design to make the protectors of “the law and property” feel slightly better than the ‘other working class.’ You get $19–22/hour and they get $40-$45/ hour. The cop feels better and more valued than you by the current capitalist system.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Taken From the SFPD website — July 2026You consider becoming a cop…or an ice agent. Hey, $40 an hour. You have bills, you have family to take care of, you have to buy food and be able to afford entertainment in late stage capitalism. You have to life.
And when you think of it, you will unlikely ever be a Peter Thiel, an Elon Musk, or a Jeff Bezos…You don’t have what it takes.
And, that is a good thing. It takes a certain level of psychopathy to amass that much wealth and feel such little responsibility for humanity to prevail and for wholesome morals. I.e. Peter Thiel, Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos, Trump, and etc. I think this is a good time to insert these screenshots from jmail below:

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Taken from jmail.world Go check out the site for yourself! Our current society ~

Going back to the police.
Globally, the police exist to protect property and to further the causes of the ‘state’ (whoever that state may be) The police do not necessarily exist to bring ‘justice.’ Neither is there a ‘justice system.’ There is a ‘legal system.’
The ‘state’ may be the San Francisco Police Department who — as of 2026- have made it their mission to arrest people for public drug use.
Weaponizing the police force against trans protesters and allies is another current mission of the San Francisco police “state.” The ‘state’ could be police in Dubai who are (technically) able to arrest a muslim woman for drinking or being disobedient to her ‘guardian.’ The ‘state’ could be the Chinese Communist Party who has many missions — one of which includes suppressing Ughyur dissident voices. The ‘state’ could be police forces whose inaction instead of action can result in harm such was the case with women like Yasmine Mohammed and Deanna Cook who the ‘state’ failed when they needed them the most.
Yasmine Mohammed was a muslim girl who — around the age of 13 — was brave enough to go to the Canadian authorities about the abuse she was enduring in her muslim home, however the judge (also a part of the state) ruled that abuse was a part of her “culture” and left her in that abusive home.
In another article I write, I will go more deeper into the concept of “culture” and how it has been weaponized and pedestalized.
Deanna Cook was a young black woman from Texas who called 911 to report that she was being attacked by her abusive ex husband. While she was essentially being beaten to death, the 911 operator (a part of the state) makes the executive decision to deprioritize her call (made it priority 2). The 911 operator was slow to respond and the police were slow to respond. And when they did arrive at her home. They did not try to force entry and check if she was okay. She was later found dead by her family.
I know I just shared with you two very tragic stories. I wanted to take you along the journey my mind was going on. I could go on with countless more. However, I think I made my point.
I am curious your thoughts on what I wrote, and which parts resonated with you. I welcome all meaningful diaologue. If you are not already subscribed to get my stories in your email inbox, please consider doing so! Email is an oldie but goodie! It ensures you can still hear my voice.
*~*
